description:
Switch the typer to emit Descr::closure_lit at MakeClosure sites and wire the new singleton through phase-1 discovery and cont_slot0_descr.

## Scope

In src/ir_typer.rs:

1. Prim::MakeClosure typing (line ~1214):
   - Old: \`Descr::arrow([any; arity], any)\`
   - New: \`Descr::closure_lit(*fn_id, capture_descrs_from_env)\`
   - capture_descrs come from env.get(captured[i]); pad with any if short.

2. Phase-1 walker (walk_spec_for_discovery, src/ir_typer.rs:725-744):
   - The "CallClosure / TailCallClosure with known target via fn_constants" case extends: also fire when the closure Var's Descr is a closure_lit (with a single resolved FnId). Pull (F, capture_key) from the singleton; build arg-side key from env.get(args); push narrow spec [capture_key..., arg_key...] to pending.
   - Multiple-singleton union: fire once per member (each pushes its own narrow spec).

3. cont_slot0_descr (src/ir_typer.rs:779-799):
   - Term::CallClosure case: today returns Descr::any() when fn_constants doesn't resolve. Now: call resolve_closure_return (from 22.9) with the closure Var's Descr + arg_descrs. Returns precise Descr for singletons, JOIN for unions, any for unresolved arrows.

## Acceptance

- cargo test --workspace green.
- fz dump on apply2.fz: apply2 specs keyed by closure_lit(double, []) and closure_lit(neg, []) rather than (any) -> any. Two specs, distinct keys.
- Bless any golden CLIFs that shift due to new spec keys. Codegen still emits cl+16 indirect dispatch — no @abi change yet, only @spec/@key strings shift in fz dump output.
- Spec count growth audited: bounded by current callsite diversity (no new axis introduced).

## Out of scope

- Codegen lowering of TailCallClosure / CallClosure to direct return_call. That is 22.11.
- 22.6 force-drop. Still gated.

## BLOCKED BY

- fz-ul4.27.22.9 [[fz-cps-seam.resolve-return]]

## UNBLOCKS

- fz-cps-seam.codegen-direct: codegen lowering to direct return_call on singleton
title: fz-cps-seam.makeclosure-emit: Prim::MakeClosure types as closure_lit; typer plumbing
